data.title: RCI Hospitality boosts buyback authorization by $20M
data.description:
• RCI Hospitality (RICK) board of directors has authorized increasing the amount available under the company's share repurchase program by an additional $20M.
  • This gives RCI around $24.8M available to buy back shares when added to the amount remaining from the previous authorization.
  • From FY16, when the company started implementing its capital allocation strategy, to date, RCI has spent almost $80M repurchasing almost 3M shares at an average of $27.88 per share, excluding the repurchase of the ADW block of shares in November 2025.

data.title: Dell sees bullish views at Mizuho on AI server demand
data.description:
Mizuho raised the price target on the shares of Dell Technologies (DELL) but reduced it for Super Micro Computer (SMCI) while discussing AI server demand.

The firm kept its Outperform rating on Dell and increased the price target to $215 from $180. Mizuho maintained its Neutral rating on Super Micro but cut its price target to $25 from $33.

"We see strong AI Server demand for 2026-27E, with NVDA 2027E DC revs tracking up >50% y/y. We note: 1) Major CSP [Cloud Service Providers] capex is now estimated at $689B for 2026E, up 64% y/y, with 2027E consensus estimated conservatively at $811B, up 18% y/y, we believe could have SIGNIFICANT upside given NVDA '27 ~50%+ y/y guide and accelerating Agentic-AI (Claude/ChatGPT/OpenClaw) driving tokens, 2) SMCI continues to be AI Server technology leader with strong AI Server CAPEX tailwinds into 2027E, NT [near term] headlines w/China could shift orders to DELL," said analysts led by Vijay Rakesh.

The analysts believe Dell could see some upside in the near term for AI servers with a potential demand/sentiment shift in the market as Dell uses its 10x AI server teams versus peers, fortress balance sheet, and estimated $85B five-quarter pipeline. 

The analysts noted that higher average selling prices, or ASPs, for PCs could offset some near-term unit headwinds as they see Dell taking PC share in 2026. The analysts also see storage upside as AI drives the demand for more storage racks with increasing parameter counts and reasoning model workloads.

"While SMCI continues to be an AI Server technology leader with an innovative and nimble team, near-term it faces some challenges with headlines of former employees' NVDA GPU sales to China, which could potentially shift some risk orders away from SMCI to key competitor DELL," said Rakesh and his team.

Shares of Dell rose about 1%, while Super Micro was largely flat but in the red premarket on Monday.

data.title: Intercontinental Exchange sees higher expenses on robust trading volumes
data.description:
Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) said on Monday it now expects higher expenses, driven by strong revenues related to robust trading volumes, with the incremental expense expected to be more than offset by the related revenues.

For the first quarter of 2026, the company expects GAAP operating expenses in the range of $1.280B-$1.290B, against the prior estimate of $1.245B-$1.255B.

Non-GAAP adjusted operating expenses are now estimated to be between $1.030B and $1.040B. The metric was estimated at $1.010B-$1.020B.

Full-year GAAP operating expenses are now expected to be in the range of $5.095B to $5.145B, vs. $5.010B-$5.075B prior. Non-GAAP adjusted operating expenses are projected to be in the range of $4.145B to $4.195B, vs. $4.075B-$4.140B prior.

Furthermore, the company sees a 45% year-over-year rise in the Q1 average daily volume, 32% in energy ADV, 29% in agriculture and metals ADV, 65% in financials ADV, 39% in NYSE cash equities ADV, and 23% in NYSE equity options ADV.

data.title: Madison Air kicks off IPO roadshow for NYSE listing
data.description:
• Madison Air (MAIR) on Monday announced the launch of its roadshow for a proposed initial public offering of 82.7 million shares, expected to price between $25.00 and $27.00 per share.
  • The company plans to list on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol 'MAIR'.
  • Proceeds from the offering are intended for repayment of certain debt.
  • A registration statement has been filed with the SEC but is not yet effective.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nano Dimension divests AME and Fabrica product lines
data.description:
• Nano Dimension (NNDM) on Monday announced the sale of its additively manufactured electronics (AME) product line and its previously discontinued Fabrica product line to Inspira Technologies (IINN) for about $12.5M.
  • The payout consists of a $2M upfront cash payment and up to $10.5M of deferred payments tied to the future performance of the product lines over the next twelve months.
  • Inspira has assumed operational control of the product lines effective immediately. 
  • NNDM -1.20% premarket to $1.64.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Praxis rises on elsunersen data for SCN2A encephalopathy in children
data.description:
• Praxis Precision Medicines (PRAX) is up ~5% in premarket trading Monday after reporting promising phase 1/2 results for elsunersen to treat pediatric patients with with early-seizure onset SCN2A developmental and epileptic encephalopathy.
  • Top-line results from the EMBRAVE Part A trial showed that elsunersen resulted in a 77% placebo-adjusted seizure reduction from baseline.
  • Other data showed that 57% of partients were seizure free for at least 28 days, and in the open-label extension, results were maintained for up to one year.
  • No drug-related severe adverse events were reported. Also, most treatment-emergent adverse events were mild to moderate.
  • Elsunersen is an antisense oligonucleotide that can decrease SCN2A gene expression, the underlying cause of early-seizure-onset SCN2A DEE.

data.title: Roth Capital Partners flags caution as markets enter Q2
data.description:
Roth Capital Partners signaled a cautious stance on equities heading into the second quarter, citing persistent geopolitical tensions and elevated cross-asset volatility. The firm noted that while the S&P 500 (SP500) has recently attempted a rebound, reclaiming its 200-day moving average remains a critical test for confirming a durable uptrend.

Until broader trend indicators strengthen, Roth views the recent market recovery as part of an oversold correction rather than the start of a sustained bullish phase. As a result, the firm is maintaining a defensive and selective sector positioning. 

Roth is currently overweight energy, materials, and utilities, favoring areas tied to commodities and defensive demand. At the same time, it holds a neutral outlook on consumer staples, industrials, technology, health care, and real estate. Meanwhile, the firm is underweight communication services, consumer discretionary, and financials, reflecting a more cautious view on economically sensitive and rate-dependent sectors. 

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL).

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI).

Consumer Staples ETFs: (XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS). 

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), (AMLP), and (IXC).

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO). 

Health Care ETFs: (XLV), (VHT), (IHI), (IXJ), (IYH), (FHLC), and (FXH). 

Industrials ETFs: (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), (IYJ), (FIDU), and (RSPN). 

Materials ETFs: (XLB), (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI), and (RSPM). 

Real Estate ETFs: (VNQ), (XLRE), (IYR), (USRT), and (HOMZ). 

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT). 

Utilities ETFs: (XLU), (VPU), (FUTY), (IDU), (FXU), and (JXI).

data.title: Energy small-cap stocks with A-grade EPS revisions
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season is set to kick off, investors are keeping a close eye on the energy sector amid a backdrop of volatile crude prices, shifting demand dynamics, and ongoing geopolitical uncertainty. 

The following list highlights small-cap energy names that have a Seeking Alpha EPS Revision Quant Grade of A- or higher, an indication that the analyst community has been raising its expectations for these companies ahead of their Q1 reports.

The current list is led by CrossAmerica Partners LP (CAPL), Kimbell Royalty Partners (KRP), Oil States International and Dorian LPG Ltd. (LPG), all of which have earned the highest possible A+ EPS revision grade. These companies represent the top performers in the energy small-cap category based on recent analyst earnings estimate revisions.

The EPS revision Quant Grade reflects the direction and magnitude of changes in analyst earnings estimates for a given stock. An A-grade indicates that analysts have been revising their earnings expectations upward, signaling positive momentum compared to the rest of the sector.

  1. CrossAmerica Partners LP (CAPL) - EPS Revision Grade: A+

  2. Kimbell Royalty Partners (KRP) - EPS Revision Grade: A+

  3. Dorian LPG Ltd. (LPG) - EPS Revision Grade: A+

  4. Oil States International, Inc. (OIS) - EPS Revision Grade: A+

  5. Flowco Holdings Inc. (FLOC) - EPS Revision Grade: A

  6. ProPetro Holding Corp. (PUMP) - EPS Revision Grade: A

  7. Bristow Group Inc. (VTOL) - EPS Revision Grade: A

  8. Global Partners LP (GLP) - EPS Revision Grade: A-

  9. Infinity Natural Resources, Inc. (INR) - EPS Revision Grade: A-

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC)

data.title: Empery Digital completes buyback of 24.6M shares under $200M plan
data.description:
• Empery Digital (EMPD) announced  on Monday it had repurchased 24.6M shares of its common stock at an average price of $5.77 per share under its previously authorized $200M share repurchase program as of April 3, 2026.
  • The company had 31.4M shares outstanding, including the potential exercise of 2.6M pre-funded warrants.
  • Separately, the company said it generated approximately $24.7M in proceeds after selling 370 bitcoin at an average price of $66,632 per token during the week ended April 3, 2026.
  • Management may use its credit facility and reduce bitcoin holdings to fund share repurchases.

data.title: OFA Group targets $15M revenue from $1B mixed-use tokenization deal
data.description:
• OFA Group (OFAL) has partnered with MD Queens Development to tokenize a ~$1B mixed-use real estate project in Long Island City via its Hearth RWA platform.
  • The company will earn ~$15M to provide blockchain tech for converting a warehouse into a residential & commercial tower.
  • The project will be tokenized early, with tokens giving investors a stake in the SPV—not direct property ownership.
  • Source: Press Release
